policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Smart Client Refresh expands core/live-update.ts from version-only notification into the always-loaded generation/config coordinator, foreground-resume heartbeat, reload-loop budget, Ask-mode action, and mandatory playback/edit safety gate. Real Pixel 5 validation added explicit hidden-visibility and Cordova pause cancellation so an Android WebView cannot poll or reload while its Activity is backgrounded. Independent review then tightened restart revision handling, source reconciliation, route/dialog safety, and required browser coverage. The legitimate core scope increase is 205 instrumented lines (2257→2462); focused state, source, lifecycle, mobile-resume, in-flight follow-up, Ask-mode, paused-playback, safety, and loop-budget tests raise the reviewed high-water by 181 covered lines (1936→2117, 85.987%). A clean Node 22.20/V8 full-suite coverage run on 2026-07-24 passed all 1429 tests across 213 files and reproduced the expanded 2462-line scope after rebasing onto current main. Retaining the one-line tolerance makes the minimum 2116/2462 (85.946%), which is stricter than both the prior 1936/2257 high-water percentage (85.778%) and its 1935/2257 tolerated floor (85.733%). coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Smart Client Refresh adds the authenticated refresh-state contract, content-addressed Canopy and process-generation Jellyfin identities, live configuration policy, explicit admin force revision, and Canopy-device registry touch. The legitimate server scope increase is 118 instrumented lines (28438→28556); focused service and controller behavior/authorization tests raise the reviewed high-water by 97 covered lines (20051→20148, 70.556%). A clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet run on 2026-07-24 passed all 1785 tests across the complete assembly. Retaining the reproduced four-line Coverlet concurrency tolerance makes the minimum 20144/28556 (70.542%), which is stricter than both the prior 20051/28438 high-water percentage (70.508%) and its 20047/28438 tolerated floor (70.494%). coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
